evals.prompt: Create a new Java Spring Boot service named 'orders-service' in /tmp/spring_eval_orders_service. Use Maven, Java 21, Spring Boot 4.x, Web MVC REST, typed app configuration, validation, Actuator health/info, production structured logging, and MockMvc tests. I do not need a database or Docker yet.
evals.expected_output: A greenfield Spring Boot service with pom.xml, root package app class, application profile files, typed @ConfigurationProperties, a small validated REST controller, ProblemDetail validation errors, Actuator health/info, and tests that do not require Docker or a database.
expectations: The project uses Maven with Spring Boot 4.x and Java 21 defaults
The web dependency uses spring-boot-starter-webmvc rather than the deprecated spring-boot-starter-web path
The main application class is in a root package above generated API/config/service packages
application.yml exposes only health and info actuator endpoints by default
Typed app configuration is implemented with @ConfigurationProperties and validation
A REST endpoint demonstrates request validation and ProblemDetail error handling
Tests include a context-load test and a MockMvc API test that require no external services

evals.prompt: Scaffold a Spring Boot API called 'billing-ledger' in /tmp/spring_eval_billing_ledger with PostgreSQL support. I want Flyway migrations, Docker Compose for local Postgres, Testcontainers integration tests, Actuator probes, and Prometheus metrics. Keep it Maven-based.
evals.expected_output: A Maven Spring Boot service with PostgreSQL datasource config, Flyway migration folder, Compose including app and postgres, Testcontainers using Spring Boot service connections, Prometheus dependency/exposure, and clear README validation commands.
expectations: The scaffold includes PostgreSQL and Flyway dependencies and a db/migration V1 migration
JPA schema generation is validate-only instead of create/drop in production configuration
Docker Compose includes a postgres service and passes datasource environment variables to the app
Tests use Testcontainers with Spring Boot @ServiceConnection for PostgreSQL
Prometheus metrics are exposed only because the prompt requested them
README states Docker is required for database integration tests

evals.prompt: Modernize the existing Spring Boot repo in /tmp/spring_eval_legacy_catalog. It is currently a Boot 3.2 Maven app with a controller returning GET /health as {"status":"ok"} and GET /items as [{"id":1,"name":"Widget"},{"id":2,"name":"Gadget"}]. Keep those exact paths and response payloads, add typed configuration, Actuator health, validation/error handling for future endpoints, tests, and a cleaner package layout. Do not invent authentication.
evals.expected_output: The existing Spring Boot app is reorganized conservatively without changing /health or /items contracts; it gains typed configuration, Actuator, validation/error handling, tests for the preserved endpoints, and no fake security system.
expectations: Existing /health and /items paths and payloads are preserved exactly
Regression tests assert the Widget/Gadget payload and original health payload
The project keeps Maven and does not force an unrelated build-tool switch
Typed configuration and Actuator are added without exposing sensitive endpoints
Validation/error handling is added for future endpoints without changing existing success responses
No authentication scaffold is invented because the prompt did not define an auth model
